title: Counsellors
description: Counsel and advise individuals with alcohol, tobacco, drug, or other problems, such as gambling and eating disorders. May counsel individuals, families, or groups or engage in prevention programs. Advise and assist students and provide educational and vocational guidance services. Diagnose and treat mental and emotional disorders, whether cognitive, affective, or behavioral, within the context of marriage and family systems. Apply psychotherapeutic and family systems theories and techniques in the delivery of services to individuals, couples, and families for the purpose of treating such diagnosed nervous and mental disorders. (Based on 17 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Interview clients individually, in families, or in groups, assessing their situations, capabilities, and problems to determine what services are required to meet their needs. Also, interview individuals or family members to compile information on social, educational, criminal, institutional, or drug history.

Develop, implement, or evaluate public education, prevention, or health promotion programs, working in collaboration with organizations, institutions, or communities. Additionally, conduct social research to advance knowledge in the social work field and plan or conduct programs to combat social problems, prevent substance abuse, or improve community health and counseling services.
Counsel family members to assist them in understanding, dealing with, and supporting clients or patients, and conduct family counseling sessions to help them develop insight into their relationships.
Participate in case conferences or staff meetings and collaborate with mental health professionals and other staff members to perform clinical assessments or develop treatment plans.
Act as liaisons between clients and medical staff, coordinating counseling efforts with mental health professionals or other health professionals, such as doctors, nurses, or social workers.
Coordinate activities with courts, probation officers, community services, or other post-treatment agencies, and monitor and record clients' progress to ensure that goals and objectives are met.

Confer with family members or others close to clients to keep them informed of treatment planning and progress, develop plans for post-treatment activities, and assess individuals' degree of drug dependency by collecting and analyzing urine samples.

Instruct others in program methods, procedures, or functions. Additionally, supervise or direct other workers providing services to clients or patients, and train or supervise student interns or new staff members.

Follow progress of discharged patients to determine treatment effectiveness, and prepare and maintain records and case files, including documentation of clients' personal data, services provided, and relevant correspondence.

Counsel clients regarding concerns such as unsatisfactory relationships, divorce, separation, child rearing, home management, or financial difficulties and assist clients in adhering to treatment plans, such as setting up appointments or providing support.

Educate clients about end-of-life symptoms and options to assist them in making informed decisions, as well as counseling individuals, groups, families, or communities regarding mental health, poverty, substance abuse, or rehabilitation issues.
Administer drug and alcohol tests, including random drug screens of offenders, to verify compliance with substance abuse treatment programs while monitoring clients' use of medications.
Monitor, evaluate, and record client progress according to measurable goals described in treatment and care plans, modifying these plans to comply with changes in clients' status as necessary.
Gather information about community mental health needs or resources that could be used in conjunction with therapy, and provide information about addiction issues and available services or programs.

Refer clients to community resources for services such as job placement, debt counseling, legal aid, and financial assistance, and encourage clients and family members to develop skills and strategies for confronting their problems in a constructive manner.
Conduct crisis interventions to help ensure the safety of the patients and others while acting as advocates for clients or patients during crisis situations.
Maintain confidentiality of records relating to clients' treatment, and complete and maintain accurate records or reports regarding the patients' histories and progress.

Review and evaluate clients' progress in relation to measurable goals described in treatment and care plans while developing and implementing individualized treatment plans addressing personal issues, family relationship problems, and destructive patterns of behavior.

Gather information using techniques such as testing, interviewing, discussion, and observation, in order to assess clients' abilities, needs, and eligibility for services, including conducting assessments of their mental and physical condition.

Coordinate or direct employee workshops or training about mental health issues and collaborate with counselors, physicians, or nurses to plan or coordinate treatment, drawing on social work experience and patient needs.
Plan and conduct structured programs of counseling, work, study, recreation, or social activities for clients to assist them in overcoming dependencies and adjusting to life.
Locate barriers to client employment and work with clients to develop strategies for overcoming these barriers, assisting them as necessary.
Visit individuals in homes or attend group meetings to provide information on agency services, requirements, or procedures, and transport clients to appointments when necessary.
Confer with clients to discuss their options and goals so that rehabilitation programs can be developed, while additionally conducting interviews with clients and reviewing records for assessment.
Plan discharge from care facility to home or other care facility while arranging for necessary evaluations for program suitability.

Counsel parents with child rearing problems, interviewing the child and family to determine further action if needed, and consult with parents and school personnel to find solutions to behavioral problems in students.
Investigate child abuse or neglect cases and take authorized protective action when necessary, addressing legal issues such as child abuse and discipline during hearings.
Provide, find, or arrange for support services, such as childcare and substance abuse treatment, to prevent more serious problems from developing.
Develop rehabilitation plans that fit clients' aptitudes, education levels, physical abilities, and career goals while assisting clients in locating housing or services for recovery.
Conduct evaluations of counseling programs and their effectiveness in helping clients progress towards defined objectives, adjusting methods as needed based on client feedback and changes in status.
Supervise other counselors and staff and provide guidance on how to improve service delivery and training.
Gather information from doctors, schools, social workers, and law enforcement personnel to make recommendations to the courts for child custody or visitation disputes, as needed.
Collect supplementary information needed to assist clients, such as employment or medical records, and maintain case history records while preparing reports as required.
Continue to increase knowledge in social work through ongoing education and training.
Serve as liaisons between students, homes, schools, family services, and other entities to facilitate support for children facing problems such as disabilities or abuse.
Advocate for clients or patients to resolve crises and ensure necessary services are provided, including monitoring their progress and treatment effectiveness.
Administer and fill out all required client-related paperwork and maintain accurate clinical records, including diagnostic and progress notes.

Counsel clients or patients, individually or in group sessions, to assist in overcoming dependencies, adjusting to life, or making changes. Evaluate clients' physical or mental condition, based on review of client information, and address personal, social, or behavioral problems affecting their situations. Refer clients to community resources or specialists as necessary.

Gather information about clients through interviews, observation, or tests, including assessing needs for assistance such as rehabilitation, financial aid, or vocational training. Collect information about clients' backgrounds and career goals to identify barriers to employment.

Develop liaisons and networks with other agencies and professionals to plan assistance for clients. Collaborate with teachers and administrators in the development and evaluation of programs and assist students with academic and career planning.
Provide crisis intervention to clients when difficult situations arise and participate in decisions regarding necessary follow-up services and support programs.
Prepare reports on clients and activities as required by administration and review their progress to determine if their needs have been met.
Develop rehabilitation or intervention programs tailored to clients' needs, establishing goals and objectives essential for their improvement and recovery.
Educate clients on topics such as career development, job search techniques, and financial literacy. Provide information on educational programs and vocational training services available.
Refer students or clients to outside counseling services and to appropriate programs in response to identified issues, including educational, social, and behavioral problems.
Attend professional meetings, educational conferences, or training workshops to improve professional skills and knowledge relevant to counselling practices.

emerging_tasks.task: Diagnose mental and emotional disorders in clients.
Guide clients in the development of skills or strategies for dealing with their problems and coping with triggering factors.
Respond to client communications by monitoring voicemail and email, returning phone calls, and making follow-up calls for missed appointments.
Teach parenting techniques to family members.
Provide teachers with notes summarizing the day's activities and feedback on any issues or events that occurred.
